title: error names must end with Error and use PascalCase
content:
# error names must end with Error and use PascalCase

When defining operation errors via `ADD_OPERATION_ERROR`:

- **`code`**: UPPERCASE_SNAKE_CASE (e.g., `"MISSING_ID"`, `"ENTRY_NOT_FOUND"`)
- **`name`**: PascalCase ending with "Error" (e.g., `"MissingIdError"`, `"EntryNotFoundError"`)
- **`description`**: Human-readable description of the error condition

```json
{
  "code": "ENTRY_NOT_FOUND",
  "name": "EntryNotFoundError",
  "description": "The referenced entry does not exist in the collection"
}
```

The "Error" suffix is required — the code generator uses it to identify error classes. Error names and IDs must be unique across all operations in the document model.

Since [[error classes in reducers are auto-imported by name reference]], the PascalCase name is what you use in reducer code to throw errors.

---

Topics:
- [[reducer patterns]]
description: Error definitions require code in UPPERCASE_SNAKE_CASE, name in PascalCase ending with Error, and a human-readable description
